title: AI-901 Mock Exam 3: Scenario Practice
description: Unofficial scenario-heavy AI-901 practice JSON for Quiz Arcade, aligned to Microsoft AI concepts and Microsoft Foundry implementation objectives.

items.prompt: A hospital chatbot must not expose patient details in prompts, logs, or responses. Which responsible AI area is most relevant?
items.answer: Privacy and security

items.prompt: An AI tool should work well for users who rely on screen readers. Which responsible AI area is most relevant?
items.answer: Inclusiveness

items.prompt: A self-service AI system gives users a route to challenge automated outcomes. Which principle does this support?
items.answer: Accountability

items.prompt: A developer publishes model limitations and tells users when content is AI-generated. Which principle does this support?
items.answer: Transparency

items.prompt: A safety-critical AI assistant is tested for harmful edge cases before release. Which principle does this support?
items.answer: Reliability and safety

items.prompt: A training dataset underrepresents one user group. What risk is most likely?
items.answer: Biased or unfair model behavior

items.prompt: Which mitigation helps reduce unfair performance differences?
items.answer: Evaluate results across demographic and contextual slices
options: Evaluate results across demographic and contextual slices

items.prompt: A model must respond with concise, factual answers based only on uploaded policies. Which instruction is best?
items.answer: Answer using only the provided policy content and cite uncertainty when not found
options: Answer using only the provided policy content and cite uncertainty when not found
Guess if the policy is missing

items.prompt: What should an app do when a grounded answer cannot be found in the supplied source material?
items.answer: Say it does not know or ask for more information
options: Say it does not know or ask for more information

items.prompt: A model response must be creative for brainstorming. Which setting might be increased carefully?
items.answer: Temperature

items.prompt: A model response must be short enough for a mobile card. Which setting helps?
items.answer: Maximum output tokens

items.prompt: A customer asks for help from a voice-enabled assistant using natural speech. What is the first conversion normally needed if using a text model flow?
items.answer: Speech to text

items.prompt: The assistant has generated a text answer and must speak it back. Which capability is needed?
items.answer: Text to speech

items.prompt: A global app needs spoken input in one language and output in another. Which capability area is relevant?
items.answer: Speech translation

items.prompt: A retail image app needs to describe what is in a photo for accessibility. Which capability fits?
items.answer: Image captioning or visual description

items.prompt: A security camera app must identify where people are in a frame. Which capability fits?
items.answer: Object detection

items.prompt: A design tool creates concept art from text. Which capability fits?
items.answer: Text-to-image generation

items.prompt: An app analyzes customer calls to extract key complaints and follow-up tasks. Which workload combination is likely?
items.answer: Speech transcription plus text analysis or content understanding
options: Speech transcription plus text analysis or content understanding

items.prompt: A legal team wants structured fields from contracts. Which capability is most relevant?
items.answer: Document information extraction

items.prompt: A media company needs to extract speakers, topics, timestamps, and key moments from long training videos. Which capability should it evaluate?
items.answer: Content Understanding for audio and video extraction
options: Content Understanding for audio and video extraction

items.prompt: A developer wants to manage models, agents, tools, monitoring, and evaluations in one Azure AI platform. What should they use?
items.answer: Microsoft Foundry

items.prompt: What does Foundry unify under a common platform?
items.answer: Agents, models, tools, management, monitoring, and enterprise controls
options: Agents, models, tools, management, monitoring, and enterprise controls

items.prompt: A Python service must connect to a Foundry project endpoint, authenticate with Azure identity, and obtain a client for model calls. Which implementation approach is appropriate?
items.answer: Create an AI project client for the Foundry project endpoint and use it to access the model client
options: Create an AI project client for the Foundry project endpoint and use it to access the model client
Call the endpoint anonymously from browser JavaScript
Paste the service account key into the user prompt
Use a spreadsheet formula to create responses

items.prompt: What does DefaultAzureCredential help with?
items.answer: Obtaining credentials from the environment, managed identity, CLI, or developer tools without hard-coding secrets
options: Obtaining credentials from the environment, managed identity, CLI, or developer tools without hard-coding secrets

items.prompt: A developer has an existing app and wants Foundry model access without moving all app code into Foundry. Which approach fits?
items.answer: Call the Foundry Responses API or SDK from the existing process
options: Call the Foundry Responses API or SDK from the existing process

items.prompt: A no-code start is preferred for an internal assistant with instructions and tools. Which agent type fits best?
items.answer: Prompt agent

items.prompt: A team has a LangGraph-based agent and wants Foundry to run it with managed scaling. Which agent type fits best?
items.answer: Hosted agent

items.prompt: Which agent component defines goals, constraints, and behavior?
items.answer: Instructions

items.prompt: Which agent component provides reasoning and language capability?
items.answer: Model

items.prompt: Which agent component lets the agent retrieve data or perform actions?
items.answer: Tools

items.prompt: An agent needs to call an internal ticketing API. Which tool pattern is most relevant?
items.answer: Custom function or API tool with managed authentication
options: Custom function or API tool with managed authentication

items.prompt: Why is tracing useful for agents?
items.answer: It shows steps, model calls, tool calls, and failures for debugging and governance
options: It shows steps, model calls, tool calls, and failures for debugging and governance
It removes the need for tests

items.prompt: What does content filtering help with?
items.answer: Reducing harmful, unsafe, or policy-violating model inputs and outputs
options: Reducing harmful, unsafe, or policy-violating model inputs and outputs

items.prompt: A model should answer from a database of product manuals. Which design pattern is appropriate?
items.answer: Retrieval-augmented generation or grounding with a search/index tool
options: Retrieval-augmented generation or grounding with a search/index tool

items.prompt: A user prompt includes instructions to reveal secrets. What should the app rely on to resist this?
items.answer: System instructions, tool access controls, and secret isolation
options: System instructions, tool access controls, and secret isolation

items.prompt: Why should a developer validate model outputs before using them in application logic?
items.answer: Model outputs can be malformed, unsafe, or inconsistent with the requested format
options: Model outputs can be malformed, unsafe, or inconsistent with the requested format

items.prompt: A JSON extraction app receives incomplete fields from a document. What should it do?
items.answer: Handle missing or low-confidence fields and prompt review when needed
options: Handle missing or low-confidence fields and prompt review when needed

items.prompt: A deployed model works in testing but has high latency in production. Which factor should be reviewed?
items.answer: Model choice, region, throughput, prompt size, and application call pattern
options: Model choice, region, throughput, prompt size, and application call pattern

items.prompt: Which choice best protects an API key used by a lightweight client app?
items.answer: Keep it server-side or use managed identity where possible
options: Keep it server-side or use managed identity where possible

items.prompt: A team needs repeatable deployment of agents and prompts. What practice helps?
items.answer: Define resources and agent configuration through code, SDK, REST, or deployment pipeline
options: Define resources and agent configuration through code, SDK, REST, or deployment pipeline
Only edit manually with no records

items.prompt: A developer wants to compare two prompts against the same deployed model before updating the production application. Where should the developer test the prompts first?
items.answer: In a Foundry playground or model testing experience
options: In a Foundry playground or model testing experience
In the Azure billing invoice page

items.prompt: A business analyst creates an agent in the Foundry portal, adds instructions, attaches a file search tool, and tests it without writing orchestration code. What type of agent is this?
items.answer: Prompt agent

items.prompt: A developer builds an app that sends customer comments to an AI service and returns sentiment, key phrases, and named entities. Which workload is being implemented?
items.answer: Text analysis

items.prompt: An insurance adjuster uploads a damaged vehicle photo and asks the application to describe visible damage. Which capability is required?
items.answer: Computer vision or multimodal visual understanding
options: Computer vision or multimodal visual understanding

items.prompt: A logistics company needs to extract container IDs from photos, invoice totals from PDFs, and spoken reference numbers from calls. Which capability area best matches this requirement?
items.answer: Information extraction across images, documents, and audio
options: Information extraction across images, documents, and audio

items.prompt: A user asks an assistant to plan a trip, check available dates through a booking API, compare options, and create an itinerary. Which workload pattern is this?
items.answer: Agentic AI

items.prompt: A deployed model must support a high-throughput classification workload with predictable, short responses. Which configuration is most important?
items.answer: Choose an appropriate low-latency model and constrain output length
options: Choose an appropriate low-latency model and constrain output length
Increase output tokens as much as possible
Use image generation for every request

items.prompt: An AI assistant gives recommendations that affect customer eligibility. The team must explain limitations, monitor outcomes, protect data, and assign human ownership for issues. What is the main design concern?
items.answer: Responsible AI governance

items.prompt: A team is preparing to deploy a Foundry-based assistant into production. Which set of activities is most appropriate before release?
items.answer: Test prompts, validate outputs, configure safety controls, monitor traces, and control access
options: Test prompts, validate outputs, configure safety controls, monitor traces, and control access
Only choose a bright UI theme
Store all secrets in frontend code

items.prompt: During a mock deployment, a model returns correct answers but sometimes includes extra prose that breaks the client parser. What should the developer do?
items.answer: Constrain the prompt or response format and validate the output before parsing
options: Constrain the prompt or response format and validate the output before parsing
Increase temperature and ignore parser errors
Remove the expected schema from the prompt
Expose the raw response directly to every downstream system
